backfill_source_binding: The repair implementation commit is immutable; a later dedicated source-seal commit contains this protocol, the source identity, registry, tests, and verification scripts. Every new receipt binds both the repair commit and exact source-seal commit plus the sealed binary hash.
provider_time_policy: Fetch and retain provider server-time evidence for every bounded kline request; observed availability is the maximum of response-complete UTC, provider HTTP Date, and associated provider server time. Availability is never backdated.
raw_response_retention: Fetch response bytes again from the approved provider. Hash exact uncompressed bytes, retain them immutably with deterministic gzip, and never read an R1P5 raw or cached body as acquisition input.
normalized_fragment_policy: Closed-schema R1P5R records bind event time, provider close time, honest present-day availability, acquisition time, receipt identity, source schema, and a new fragment hash.
receipt_chain_policy: Use only the new R1P5R chain ID and all-zero genesis. Each receipt binds prior and resulting chain hashes, repair/source-seal commits, binary, protocol, registry, provider time, raw response, normalized fragment, and durable ledger evidence.
daily_partition_policy: UTC symbol/day; completeness requires the exact 1,440 distinct monotonically ordered minute opens plus complete new raw, fragment, receipt, provider-time, and chain evidence. Row count alone is insufficient.
checkpoint_policy: Create immutable r1p5r-checkpoint generations that bind exact eligible scope, durable cutoff, all nine symbols, new chain, a valid historical P4 live terminal, repair/source-seal/binary/protocol/schema/policy/manifest/receipt/ledger/registry identities, every partition, gaps, conflicts, timestamp, and checkpoint hash. New live additions require another generation; old checkpoint identities are rejected.
conflict_and_duplicate_policy: Byte-equivalent overlap within R1P5R is idempotent. Differing content for one symbol/minute fails closed. There is no last-write-wins or old-evidence allowlist.
live_backfill_concurrency: R1P5R uses its own storage namespace, lock, state, cursor, receipt chain, and binary. It never takes or mutates the P4 live collector lock, state, receipt chain, or cursor.
readiness_policy: Structural readiness requires at least 180 consecutive all-symbol UNEXPOSED_PIT_EVIDENCE_COMPLETE UTC days, a valid new checkpoint, the new authority, and a valid P4 live chain. The watcher reads no candidate or RIF state.
candidate_research_prohibition: Do not invoke candidate code, inspect candidate artifacts, calculate outcomes, read research partitions, or create real RIF state during collection, checkpointing, or readiness.
abandoned_evidence_prohibition: Do not import any old R1P5 raw response, fragment, receipt, cursor, manifest, partition, chain entry, checkpoint, binary, or cached HTTP body. Old evidence may be read only by isolated forensic verification commands.
